codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly achieves all feature requirements. Content surface margins are set to 0 so the content extends full-screen behind both chrome overlays. Title bar and status bar use alpha=220 (within the 180-220 expected range). Title bar displays 'Document OS' branding. Status bar displays character count and image dimensions (mode-dependent). Chrome is composited via composite_surfaces/blit_blend over content. 4 well-targeted compositing tests verify alpha bleed-through behavior. All 711 tests pass. Visual verification via QEMU screenshots confirmed translucency, text legibility, and chrome distinctness.

issues.description: Incomplete content surface clearing on first frame. With TEXT_INSET_TOP=4, the first render clears only ~44px (4 + 0 + 2*20) of the full-screen content surface via clear_end_y. The remainder stays as vec![0u8; ...] = transparent black (BGRA 0,0,0,0). When composited, transparent content pixels show the background (18,18,26) instead of the content bg (24,24,36). The colors are nearly identical so this is visually imperceptible, but it means the translucency effect through the status bar on initial boot technically shows background color rather than content color. A full-surface clear on first render (when PREV_LAST_Y == 0) would fix this.

TEXT_INSET_BOTTOM=4 is semantically misleading. The comment says 'Text starts near the top of the content surface so that document content is genuinely visible through the translucent chrome overlays' but TEXT_INSET_BOTTOM=4 only controls a 4px fill at the very bottom of the screen, not the status bar region. The actual constraint keeping text out of the status bar is max_text_y_in_content() which uses LINE_H + TEXT_INSET_BOTTOM (~24px). Since STATUS_BAR_H=28, text can render within 4px of the status bar's top edge — which is correct for the translucency effect but the constant name suggests it controls status bar clearance. A comment clarifying the relationship between TEXT_INSET_BOTTOM, max_text_y_in_content, and STATUS_BAR_H would help.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The architecture.md library file states 'No rounded rects, no blur, no gradients yet' but the drawing library already has fill_gradient_v (used for drop shadows since at least the multi-surface compositor feature). This pre-existing inaccuracy was not introduced by this worker but is worth correcting.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md final line says 'No rounded rects, no blur, no gradients yet'. system/services/compositor/main.rs uses surf.fill_gradient_v() in render_title_shadow and render_status_shadow.

summary: Pass. The translucent chrome feature is correctly implemented: content surface extends full-screen (margins=0), chrome overlays use alpha=220, content is genuinely visible through both title and status bars, branding and character count display as specified. 4 new compositing tests cover the alpha bleed-through behavior. Two non-blocking issues noted: (1) first-frame content surface is mostly transparent due to incremental clearing optimization — invisible in practice since bg colors differ by only 6 units per channel, (2) TEXT_INSET_BOTTOM naming could be clearer about its relationship to status bar height. Worker followed the skill procedure, ran tests (711 pass), built cleanly, and performed QEMU visual verification.
